<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 8 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- llms.txt and context.md are generated outputs that should not be hand-edited — document this clearly to prevent accidental manual edits being overwritten on next analyze run
- vitest.config.ts at root scope implies a monorepo-level test runner; verify it correctly scopes to only the rex package tests and does not inadvertently pick up sibling package tests
- README.md and image assets (Rex-F.png, Rex.png) have no import relationships with source code, which is correct — keep documentation artifacts separate from the build graph
- Zero cohesion and coupling reflect that this zone contains only configuration and documentation files with no import edges — expected for a project root zone.
- context.md and llms.txt are sourcevision-generated AI context files; their presence in the root zone confirms they are correctly co-located with the package they describe.
- llms.txt and context.md are generated outputs co-located in this zone alongside package.json and tsconfig.json — their presence here is correct, but the zone name 'project-configuration' is misleading since it also contains AI-readable generated artifacts that serve a runtime documentation role, not a configuration role.
- The project-configuration zone conflates two distinct artifact categories: build/config files (package.json, tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts) and AI-context outputs (llms.txt, context.md). These have different lifecycles and different consumers — config files are read by tooling, AI context files are consumed by LLMs. This distinction is invisible in the current zone grouping.

</insights>
